title: We Shall Surely Die
content:
We shall surely die:
    Must we needs grow old?
    Grow old and cold,
    And we know not why?

    O, the By-and-By,
    And the tale that's told!
    We shall surely die:
    Must we needs grow old?

    Grow old and sigh,
    Grudge and withhold,
    Resent and scold? . . .
    Not you and I?
    We shall surely die!
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Ernest Henley, titled "We Shall Surely Die", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
